engineGuide.h2: Dodge Challenger Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Dodge Challenger across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 5.7L Petrol
entries.history: The definitive R/T engine, bringing 370hp HEMI power to the modern Challenger with MDS cylinder deactivation for efficiency.
commonFailures: Valve seat dropping - exhaust seats can drop into cylinder from 60k miles, causing catastrophic failure
MDS lifter collapse - cylinder deactivation lifters fail, damaging camshaft and requiring full rebuild
Oil consumption increase - worn rings lead to excessive oil burn on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for EZH Dodge Challenger engine replacement

entries.title: 5.7L Petrol
entries.history: Early-production 5.7L HEMI code, largely superseded by EZH but still found in 2008-2010 UK imports.
commonFailures: Valve seat dropping - same risk as EZH, particularly on unmodified high-mileage examples
Oil pump wear - early units may exhibit reduced oil pressure after 80k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for EZD Dodge Challenger engine replacement

entries.title: 5.7L Petrol
entries.history: Late-production 5.7L HEMI with minor revisions, found in final-year R/T models before Challenger discontinuation.
commonFailures: MDS lifter failure - remains the dominant failure mode across all 5.7L HEMI applications
Oil consumption - piston ring wear still prevalent on hard-driven examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for EZF Dodge Challenger engine replacement

entries.title: 3.6L Petrol
entries.history: The entry-level Challenger engine, delivering 305hp from a naturally aspirated V6 with proven reliability when maintained.
compatibleVariants: SXT, GT, SXT Plus, SXT Blacktop
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure - plastic tensioners wear, causing chain slap and potential valve damage
Cylinder head cracking - known issue on pre-2016 units, leading to coolant loss
Oil cooler housing leaks - gasket failure causes oil/coolant mixing
entries.cta: Get quotes for ERB Dodge Challenger engine replacement

entries.title: 3.6L Petrol
entries.history: Revised Pentastar code with updated cylinder heads and improved oiling, found in later SXT and GT models.
commonFailures: Timing chain guide wear - improved but still a known failure point on high-mileage units
Oil cooler housing leaks - persists across all Pentastar revisions
entries.cta: Get quotes for ERC Dodge Challenger engine replacement

entries.title: 6.4L Petrol
entries.history: The iconic 392 cubic inch HEMI, delivering 485hp to Scat Pack and T/A variants - a favourite for enthusiasts and tuners.
compatibleVariants: R/T Scat Pack, 392 HEMI, T/A 392, Widebody 392
commonFailures: Valve seat dropping - more prevalent under hard driving or track use
Piston ring land failure - aggressive tuning or poor fuel can cause collapse
Oil pump pickup clogging - debris from wear restricts flow, risking bearing failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for ESG Dodge Challenger engine replacement

entries.title: 6.2L Supercharged Petrol
entries.history: The Hellcat's supercharged 6.2L HEMI, producing 707-717hp - a specialist engine requiring expert handling for replacement.
commonFailures: Supercharger snout seal failure - oil leaks from supercharger front seal, common on high-boost applications
Piston ring failure - high cylinder pressures accelerate ring wear on track-driven cars
Heat exchanger clogging - reduced cooling efficiency leads to timing pull and power loss
entries.cta: Get quotes for ESJ Dodge Challenger engine replacement

entries.title: 6.2L High-Output Petrol
entries.history: The Demon and Super Stock's high-output variant, producing 807-840hp with upgraded internals for drag racing.
compatibleVariants: SRT Demon, SRT Super Stock, Jailbreak (high-output)
commonFailures: Piston ring land failure - extreme cylinder pressures demand frequent inspection on track cars
Supercharger coupling wear - high-torque applications accelerate coupling fatigue
Oil pump drive failure - high-RPM drag use can shear the oil pump drive gear
entries.cta: Get quotes for ESH Dodge Challenger engine replacement

Every rebuilt engine comes with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

commonProblems.h2: Dodge Challenger Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Dodge Challenger's HEMI V8 and Pentastar V6 engines are powerful but have known failure points that drive UK replacement demand. The 5.7L HEMI (EZH) suffers valve seat dropping and MDS lifter failure, while the 3.6L Pentastar (ERB) is prone to timing chain tensioner wear. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Valve Seat Dropping - Why 5.7L & 6.4L HEMI Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Dodge Challenger R/T, R/T Shaker, T/A, Scat Pack (2008-2023, EZH/EZD/EZF/ESG)
problems.rootCause: Exhaust valve seats can loosen and drop into the cylinder due to thermal cycling and inadequate retention, causing catastrophic piston and head damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Borescope inspection of valve seats, compression test, oil analysis
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable before failure; cannot prevent dropped seats once wear begins

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, install upgraded valve seat inserts, machine surface, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses one cylinder; other seats may still fail later

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt long block with upgraded valve seats, MDS delete option, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Most cost-effective for high-mileage cars; eliminates recurrence risk

problems.recommendation: On a Dodge Challenger R/T worth £15,000-£25,000, a full rebuilt engine with upgraded valve seats offers better long-term value than repeated head repairs. We recommend replacement if mileage exceeds 80k or if multiple cylinders show seat wear.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EZH HEMI engine prices for your Dodge Challenger

problems.h4: MDS Lifter Failure - The "HEMI Tick" That Destroys Camshafts
problems.affectedModels: Dodge Challenger R/T, GT, Scat Pack (2008-2023, all 5.7L & 6.4L HEMI with MDS)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic lifters in the Multi-Displacement System collapse due to oil starvation or wear, causing the camshaft lobe to grind and sending metal debris through the engine.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace collapsed lifters, inspect cam, flush oil system
repairOptions.longevity: High risk of recurrence; cam may already be damaged

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace camshaft, all lifters, timing components, MDS solenoids
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses immediate damage but leaves worn block internals

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt HEMI with MDS Delete
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded lifters, optional MDS delete kit
repairOptions.longevity: Eliminates MDS failure risk entirely; best for high-mileage or performance use

problems.recommendation: If you hear the characteristic "tick" above 60k miles, budget for full replacement. The cost of camshaft repair often approaches a rebuilt engine, and MDS delete kits prevent recurrence.
problems.cta: Get quotes for MDS-delete rebuilt HEMI engines for your Challenger

problems.h4: Pentastar Timing Chain Failure - Why 3.6L V6 Engines Rattle
problems.rootCause: Plastic timing chain tensioners and guides wear prematurely, causing chain slap, misalignment, and potential valve-to-piston contact if the chain skips.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace front tensioner, inspect chain wear, reset timing
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if caught early before guide wear or chain stretch

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, guides, tensioners, phasers; reset timing
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses wear but leaves aged engine internals; suitable for <80k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt long block with updated timing components, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable for high-mileage SXT/GT; eliminates timing and head cracking risks

problems.recommendation: On a Challenger SXT worth £10,000-£18,000, a rebuilt Pentastar engine with updated timing components offers peace of mind and avoids the risk of valve damage from chain skip.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt ERB Pentastar engine prices for your Dodge Challenger

problems.h4: Oil Consumption & Piston Ring Wear - The Silent HEMI Killer
problems.affectedModels: Dodge Challenger R/T, Scat Pack (2008-2023, EZH/ESG)
problems.rootCause: Piston rings wear or stick due to carbon buildup, allowing oil to enter combustion chambers, leading to fouled plugs, catalytic damage, and eventual seizure.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Top-End Clean & Ring Stick Treatment
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Chemical decarbonisation, oil flush, high-mileage oil
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; does not address worn rings

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove heads, replace rings, hone cylinders, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Labour-intensive; risk of head gasket issues on reassembly

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt HEMI with Upgraded Rings
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded ring design, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Most cost-effective for high-mileage cars; eliminates recurrence

problems.recommendation: If your HEMI is burning over 1L per 1,000 miles, replacement is more economical than ring replacement. Upgraded rings in rebuilt units prevent repeat failures.
problems.cta: Find rebuilt HEMI engines with upgraded rings for your Challenger

problems.h4: Cylinder Head Cracking - Pentastar's Hidden Weakness
problems.affectedModels: Dodge Challenger SXT, GT (2011-2015, ERB early production)
problems.rootCause: Thermal stress and casting flaws in early Pentastar heads cause cracks between valve seats, leading to coolant loss, overheating, and potential head warpage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace gasket, pressure test heads, refill coolant
repairOptions.longevity: Does not address cracked heads; high recurrence risk

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and replace cracked head(s), machine surface, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses known weak point but leaves aged block internals

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Pentastar with Updated Heads
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with revised cylinder heads, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Eliminates cracking risk; best for pre-2016 Challengers over 70k miles

problems.recommendation: For pre-2016 Challenger SXT/GT models showing coolant loss, a rebuilt engine with updated heads is more reliable than repeated head repairs.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt Pentastar engines with updated heads

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Dodge Challenger
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're dealing with a ticking HEMI, a rattling Pentastar, or catastrophic valve seat failure, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Dodge Challenger specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components to address known failure points, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Dodge Challenger Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Dodge Challenger and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Dodge Challenger Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Dodge Challenger in the UK is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Dodge Challenger 3.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.6L Pentastar V6 powered entry-level SXT and GT Challengers from 2011 onwards, offering 305hp with reasonable fuel economy for a muscle car. Known for timing chain tensioner wear on pre-2016 units, but generally reliable with proper maintenance.
commonFailurePoints: cylinder head cracking on early units
items.cta: Compare Dodge Challenger 3.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Dodge Challenger 5.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: The iconic 5.7L HEMI V8 defined the R/T Challenger, delivering 370-375hp with MDS cylinder deactivation. Dominates UK replacement demand due to valve seat dropping and MDS lifter failure patterns on high-mileage examples.
items.cta: Compare Dodge Challenger 5.7L petrol engine prices

items.title: Dodge Challenger 6.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 392 cubic inch (6.4L) HEMI brought 485hp to Scat Pack and T/A variants, appealing to enthusiasts and tuners. Higher performance demands increase stress on valve seats and pistons, particularly on track-driven examples.
commonFailurePoints: Valve seat dropping under hard use
items.cta: Compare Dodge Challenger 6.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Dodge Challenger 6.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The supercharged 6.2L HEMI powered Hellcat, Demon and Super Stock variants, producing 707-840hp. Specialist engines requiring expert handling; replacement costs reflect performance-grade components and limited UK supplier availability.
commonFailurePoints: oil pump drive wear on drag cars

families: EZH - 5.7L HEMI for R/T models; valve seat and MDS lifter failure risks

engineYears.h2: Dodge Challenger Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Dodge Challenger's UK production run, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The modern Challenger returned with retro styling, offering a 3.5L V6 base engine and the iconic 5.7L HEMI in R/T trim.
keyChanges: Challenger nameplate revived on LX platform shared with Charger
5.7L HEMI (EZD) introduced with MDS cylinder deactivation
Early UK imports primarily R/T models with manual or automatic transmission
knownFor: Strong aftermarket support for 5.7L HEMI from launch
Early EZD engines share valve seat risks with later EZH codes
3.5L V6 largely phased out of UK imports after 2010
years.cta: Get quotes for 2008-2010 Dodge Challenger replacement engines

years.description: The 3.6L Pentastar V6 replaced the 3.5L, while the 5.7L HEMI received the updated EZH code with minor revisions.
keyChanges: 3.6L Pentastar (ERB) introduced for SXT and GT variants
5.7L HEMI updated to EZH code with improved oiling and MDS calibration
6.4L 392 HEMI (ESG) launched for Scat Pack and 392 models
knownFor: Pentastar timing chain tensioner wear emerges on high-mileage examples
EZH HEMI valve seat dropping becomes documented failure pattern
392 HEMI gains enthusiast following for tuning potential
years.cta: Get quotes for 2011-2014 Dodge Challenger replacement engines
years.ticker: Dodge Challenger R/T engine replacement • Dodge Challenger 392 HEMI cost • Pentastar timing chain fix

years.description: The 2015 facelift brought updated styling, electronics, and the game-changing supercharged 6.2L HEMI in Hellcat form.
keyChanges: Exterior and interior refresh with LED lighting and Uconnect 8.4
6.2L supercharged HEMI (ESJ) launched for SRT Hellcat (707hp)
Pentastar updated to ERC code with revised cylinder heads
knownFor: Hellcat establishes new performance benchmark for UK imports
EZH HEMI MDS lifter failure reports increase on high-mileage R/T models
Pentastar head cracking issues largely resolved in ERC revision
years.cta: Get quotes for 2015-2018 Dodge Challenger replacement engines
years.ticker: Hellcat engine replacement cost • Scat Pack 6.4L rebuild • R/T HEMI lifter tick fix

years.description: The Challenger's final years introduced the 840hp Demon, Super Stock, and Jailbreak editions before production ended in 2023.
keyChanges: 6.2L High-Output HEMI (ESH) launched for Demon (840hp) and Super Stock (807hp)
Widebody packages become available across Scat Pack and Hellcat trims
"Last Call" special editions mark end of Challenger production
mainEngines: ESJ/ESH - 6.2L supercharged petrol (Hellcat, Demon, Super Stock)
knownFor: Demon and Super Stock engines require specialist handling for replacement
Final-year EZF HEMI codes include minor durability revisions
UK supply chain for late-model Challengers remains active via import specialists
years.cta: Get quotes for 2019-2023 Dodge Challenger replacement engines
years.ticker: Demon 6.2L replacement • Jailbreak engine compatibility • Widebody Scat Pack rebuild cost

faq.h2: Dodge Challenger Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Dodge Challenger engine in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Dodge Challenger engine prices range from £2,200 for a 3.6L Pentastar to £15,000+ for a supercharged Hellcat unit, depending on engine code, variant and whether you choose supply-only or full supply & fit.
keyPoints: 3.6L Pentastar rebuilt: £2,200-£4,200 supply only
5.7L HEMI rebuilt: £3,500-£6,500 supply only
6.4L 392 HEMI rebuilt: £5,500-£9,500 supply only
Labour for supply & fit adds £800-£2,200 depending on garage rates
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Dodge Challenger engine prices for your variant

items.question: Where can I buy a replacement Dodge Challenger engine in the UK?
items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with vetted UK specialists who stock or source rebuilt, reconditioned and used Dodge Challenger engines, including HEMI and Pentastar units with warranty coverage.
keyPoints: Network includes American car specialists familiar with HEMI quirks
All suppliers offer minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
Nationwide delivery available; many offer supply & fit services
Enter your registration to get tailored quotes from multiple suppliers
items.cta: Get quotes from UK Dodge Challenger engine specialists today

items.question: Can I swap a Hellcat engine into my V6 Challenger?
items.answer: While technically possible, a Hellcat engine swap into a V6 Challenger requires extensive modifications to wiring, ECU, cooling and drivetrain, often costing more than the engine itself.
keyPoints: Hellcat ESJ/ESH engines need supercharger-specific ECU and wiring
Cooling system, transmission and rear axle require upgrades
UK vehicle approval (IVA) may be needed for major powertrain changes
Most owners find replacing the original engine more cost-effective
items.cta: Enquire about Dodge Challenger engine compatibility for your variant

items.question: What engines are compatible with my Dodge Challenger?
items.answer: Engine compatibility depends on your Challenger's generation and original engine code; 5.7L HEMI codes (EZH/EZD) generally interchange within 2008-2023 R/T models, while Pentastar codes (ERB/ERC) fit SXT/GT variants.
keyPoints: Always match engine code to your VIN for guaranteed fitment
2015+ facelift models have updated electronics requiring code-matched ECUs
6.4L ESG and 6.2L ESJ engines are not direct swaps for 5.7L applications
Our registration lookup instantly identifies compatible replacement options
items.cta: Identify your exact Dodge Challenger engine code via registration

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Dodge Challenger?
items.answer: For most Challengers valued over £12,000, replacing a failed engine with a rebuilt unit is more economical than scrapping, especially when the replacement includes upgraded components to prevent repeat failures.
keyPoints: Rebuilt HEMI engines with upgraded valve seats prevent recurrence
Pentastar replacements with updated timing components offer long-term reliability
Supply & fit packages simplify the process for non-specialist owners
Compare multiple quotes to find the best value for your budget
items.cta: Calculate whether engine replacement makes sense for your Challenger

items.question: What warranty comes with a replacement Dodge Challenger engine?
items.answer: All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists, with options to extend coverage for high-performance variants.
keyPoints: Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
High-output Hellcat/Demon units may have specialist warranty terms
Keep service records and use recommended oil to maintain coverage
Confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before ordering
items.cta: View warranty options for your Dodge Challenger engine replacement

items.question: How long does a Dodge Challenger engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engines typically dispatch within 3-7 working days; full supply & fit installations require 2-4 days at a specialist garage, depending on parts availability and workshop schedule.
keyPoints: Common codes like EZH and ERB often have UK stock for faster dispatch
Performance variants (ESJ/ESH) may require longer lead times
Pre-book supply & fit slots to minimise vehicle downtime
Ask about courtesy car options during installation
items.cta: Check availability and lead times for your Dodge Challenger engine

items.question: What's the price difference between 5.7L and 6.4L HEMI replacement?
items.answer: A rebuilt 6.4L ESG HEMI typically costs £2,000-£3,000 more than a 5.7L EZH unit due to higher-performance internals, lower production volumes and specialist handling requirements.
keyPoints: 6.4L engines have stronger internals but higher failure costs under hard use
Scat Pack owners often opt for upgraded components during rebuild
Supply & fit labour is similar for both displacements
Compare quotes for both to assess value for your driving style
items.cta: Compare 5.7L vs 6.4L HEMI replacement costs for your Challenger

items.question: What are the most common Dodge Challenger engine problems?
items.answer: The 5.7L/6.4L HEMI suffers valve seat dropping and MDS lifter failure, while the 3.6L Pentastar is prone to timing chain tensioner wear and early-model head cracking.
keyPoints: HEMI valve seat failure typically occurs 60k-100k miles
MDS lifter collapse causes the characteristic "HEMI tick"
Pentastar timing issues emerge 70k-110k miles on pre-2016 units
Rebuilt engines with upgraded components address these known failures
items.cta: Find rebuilt Dodge Challenger engines with failure-point upgrades

items.question: What oil should I use in my Dodge Challenger engine to prevent failures?
items.answer: Use full-synthetic 5W-20 oil meeting Chrysler MS-6395 specification for all HEMI and Pentastar engines; change every 5,000-7,500 miles to reduce wear on valve seats, lifters and timing components.
keyPoints: Correct oil viscosity maintains MDS system pressure and HEMI longevity
Frequent changes reduce carbon buildup that contributes to ring stick
Use OEM or approved aftermarket filters to ensure proper flow
Keep service records to maintain warranty coverage on rebuilt units
items.cta: Get quotes for Dodge Challenger engines with recommended maintenance history
